sections.summary: Non‑substantive heading: title indicating this document is the Codex standard for longans.
Non‑substantive entry: document identifier CXS 220-1999 (Codex standard code).
Records adoption and amendment history: standard adopted in 1999 and amended in 2005 and 2011.

This standard applies to commercial varieties of longans (Dimocarpus longan Lour., Sapindaceae) supplied fresh to the consumer after preparation and packaging, and expressly excludes longans intended for industrial processing.

Sets minimum quality requirements (e.g., whole, sound, clean, practically free of pests and pest damage, free of abnormal external moisture, foreign smell/taste, temperature damage and pronounced blemishes; appropriate ripeness and ability to withstand transport) and classifies longans into three classes (Extra, Class I, Class II) with progressively relaxed defect tolerances, including specific permitted slight skin defects for Class I and II.

Minimum requirements require longans to be whole, sound (no rotting), clean, practically free of pests and pest damage, free of abnormal external moisture, foreign smell/taste, temperature damage and pronounced blemishes, and to have reached appropriate development and ripeness to withstand transport and arrive in satisfactory condition; skin and flesh colour may vary by variety and skin may be lighter if treated with sulphur dioxide gas.
Non‑substantive heading stating that longans are classified into three classes (Extra, Class I and Class II).

Extra class: longans must be of superior quality, characteristic of the variety and free of defects except for very slight superficial defects that do not affect general appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation in the package.

Class I: longans must be of good quality and characteristic of the variety; slight defects (e.g., bruising, scratches or other mechanical damage) are allowed provided they do not affect general appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation and do not exceed a total area of 0.5 cm².

Class II: includes longans that do not qualify for higher classes but meet the minimum requirements, with allowed defects such as skin bruising or scratches not exceeding a total area of 0.5 cm²; a footnote permits smells caused by conservation agents when used in compliance with applicable regulations.

Size is determined by number of fruits per kilogram or equatorial diameter with size codes 1–5 as follows: Code 1: <85 fruits/kg or diameter >28 mm; Code 2: 85–94 or >27–28 mm; Code 3: 95–104 or >26–27 mm; Code 4: 105–114 or >25–26 mm; Code 5: >115 or 24–25 mm.
Allows tolerances in each package for longans not satisfying the indicated class, with detailed quality tolerances by class and size tolerances specified for all classes.

Sets numerical quality tolerances for longans by class: Extra allows deviations meeting Class I (or its tolerances), Class I allows deviations meeting Class II (or its tolerances), and Class II permits up to 10% not meeting class or minimum requirements except produce unfit for consumption.
Permits up to 5% by number or weight of longans in the 'Extra' class to fail to meet Extra requirements provided they meet Class I or, exceptionally, fall within Class I tolerances.
Permits up to 10% by number or weight of longans in Class I to fail to meet Class I requirements provided they meet Class II or, exceptionally, fall within Class II tolerances.

Permits up to 10% by number or weight of longans in Class II to fail to meet the class or minimum requirements, excluding produce affected by rotting or other deterioration that renders it unfit for consumption.
Size tolerances for all classes: up to 20% by number or weight may be the size immediately above and/or below the indicated size for fruit sold in bunches, and up to 10% for fruit sold individually.

Specifies presentation and packaging requirements for longans: packages must be uniform by origin/variety/quality/size and representative, packed to protect produce using new or food‑grade recycled materials, and containers must meet hygiene, ventilation and resistance standards; longans may be presented individually (pedicel cut at first knot, stalk ≤5 mm) or in bunches (each stem with ≥3 fruits, branch ≤15 cm, and max 10% detached fruit by number or weight).
Requires each package of longans to be uniform and contain only fruit of the same origin, variety, quality and size, with the visible contents representative of the whole.

Requires longans to be packed so as to protect the produce; internal packaging materials must be new (including food‑grade recycled), clean and of a quality that avoids external or internal damage, printed materials must use non‑toxic ink/glue, and packing must comply with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44‑1995).
Containers for longans must provide adequate quality, hygiene, ventilation and strength for handling, shipping and preservation and must be free of foreign matter and odours.
States longans must be presented in one of the specified forms (e.g., individually or in bunches) and notes that ‘new’ packaging may include recycled food‑grade material.
When packed individually, the longan pedicel must be cut at the first knot and the stalk must not project more than 5 mm beyond the top of the fruit.
When presented in bunches, each stem must bear at least three longans, the branch must not exceed 15 cm, and up to 10% (by number or weight) of detached fruit is allowed per package.

Specifies marking/label requirements: consumer packages follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985) plus specific rules, and non‑retail containers must bear legible/indelible particulars on the same side (exporter/packer identification, produce name/variety if not visible, country/district of origin, commercial identification such as class and size, optional net weight) with an optional official inspection mark or those particulars may appear in accompanying documents.

Heading stating that consumer package labelling must comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985) and that additional specific provisions apply (details given elsewhere).
Requires packages in which the longans are not visible externally to be labelled with the name of the produce and optionally the variety.

For non-retail containers, each package must bear specified particulars grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked and visible from the outside or provided in accompanying shipment documents.
Requires the name and address of the exporter, packer and/or dispatcher to appear on the package, with an optional identification code.
If contents are not visible, the package must state the name of the produce, with the variety name optional.
The package must indicate the country of origin and may optionally include the district or national/regional/local place name where grown.
Commercial identification must include class, size (size code or minimum and maximum diameter in millimetres) and may optionally show net weight.
Non-substantive heading noting an optional official inspection mark; no details or requirements are provided in this section.
Produce covered by this Standard must comply with the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

sections.section: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
sections.summary: The produce must comply with the maximum levels specified in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.section: 7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.summary: Obligates that the produce covered by this Standard must comply with the Codex Alimentarius Commission's maximum residue limits (MRLs) for pesticides.

Hygiene provisions recommending that produce be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003) and other relevant Codex texts, and that the produce comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

sections.section: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.
sections.summary: Recommendation that produce be prepared and handled in accordance with the relevant sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other applicable Codex hygienic practice texts.

sections.section: 8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
sections.summary: States that the produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
